name: Greenpath, Inc.
description_short: HUD and NFCC-certified non-profit housing counselor offering free foreclosure prevention, mortgage delinquency, and rental counseling services.
description_long:
GreenPath Financial Wellness is a non-profit credit counseling organization that specializes in housing-related financial guidance. The organization employs HUD and NFCC-certified counselors who provide expert advice on homeownership and rental housing matters. GreenPath operates a dedicated housing services division accessible through their website and phone lines, serving both homeowners and renters with no appointment required.

GreenPath offers a range of housing services including free foreclosure prevention counseling, mortgage delinquency counseling, rental assistance counseling, homebuyer education, reverse mortgage counseling, and mortgage modification support. Their counselors can contact lenders on behalf of clients to explore available options and programs. Most core services—foreclosure prevention, mortgage delinquency, and rental counseling—are completely free. The organization charges modest fees only for pre-purchase counseling ($150, often waived for credit union members) and reverse mortgage counseling ($199).

What distinguishes GreenPath is their certified counselor network, zero-cost model for crisis housing situations, and explicit commitment to client confidentiality without credit bureau reporting. They operate extended hours including evenings, weekends, and overnight availability, accommodating various schedules. The organization does not guarantee mortgage modification outcomes but transparently explains they can only facilitate conversations with servicers based on what programs borrowers qualify for. Their website includes accessibility features and multilingual support (Spanish).

GreenPath is best suited for homeowners facing foreclosure, those with mortgage payment difficulties, prospective homebuyers seeking education, renters needing guidance, and seniors exploring reverse mortgages. The primary caveat is that they provide counseling and facilitation rather than direct financial solutions—outcomes depend on lender willingness and borrower eligibility for state/federal programs.
pros: Free foreclosure prevention and mortgage delinquency counseling—no cost for crisis housing situations
HUD and NFCC-certified counselors providing credible, expert guidance
Extended availability including nights, weekends, and no-appointment walk-in counseling
Explicit confidentiality guarantee with no credit bureau reporting
Free lender contact and mortgage modification facilitation for eligible clients
Credit union partnership programs that waive pre-purchase counseling fees for members
Comprehensive services covering homebuyers, homeowners, renters, and seniors
cons: Cannot guarantee mortgage modification or refinancing outcomes—dependent on lender discretion
Pre-purchase counseling charges $150 fee (though credit union members may avoid this)
Reverse mortgage counseling costs $199, which may deter price-sensitive seniors
Counseling is advisory only; they cannot directly negotiate loan terms or force lender action
Limited detail on their success rates or case outcomes publicly available
best_for: Homeowners facing foreclosure or mortgage delinquency seeking free intervention
Prospective homebuyers wanting education before purchase (especially credit union members)
Renters experiencing housing instability or eviction risk
Seniors exploring reverse mortgages who want expert cost and benefit explanation
diagnosis: GreenPath is ideal for homeowners in foreclosure or mortgage payment trouble, renters facing instability, and first-time homebuyers—all seeking free or low-cost certified counseling. The main caveat is that GreenPath provides expert guidance and lender communication but cannot directly modify loans or guarantee outcomes; success depends on what lenders and government programs will approve.
services: Pre-purchase homebuyer counseling and education ($150, sometimes waived)
Federal and state housing program eligibility assessment
Phone and walk-in counseling (no appointment required)
Referrals to additional housing resources (HPF, Eviction Lab, Just Shelter)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
